commit.message:
fix: resolve TOCTOU race condition in RateLimiter (#921)

The Allow(), GetRemainingTokens(), and AllowWebSocketMessage() methods
had a time-of-check-time-of-use race: the outer map lock was released
before the per-bucket mutex was acquired, allowing cleanupOldBuckets()
to delete the bucket in between. Under high concurrency this could
cause rate limiting to silently fail or panic on a freed bucket.

Fix: acquire the bucket/limiter mutex while still holding the outer
lock, then release the outer lock. This maintains consistent lock
ordering (outer -> bucket) matching cleanupOldBuckets(), preventing
deadlocks.

Added concurrent tests with race-detector coverage for all three
affected methods, including a test that simulates the exact TOCTOU
scenario with simultaneous Allow() and cleanup calls.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add graceful shutdown to WebSocket Hub (#925)

Hub.Run() now accepts context.Context and selects on ctx.Done() and
a new done channel so the goroutine exits cleanly on server shutdown.

Changes:
- Add done channel to WebSocketHub; close it via Shutdown()
- Run(ctx) returns on context cancellation or Shutdown(), draining all
  client send channels first
- readPump/secureReadPump use select on done to avoid blocking on
  unregister/broadcast sends after the hub stops
- BroadcastMessage checks done to drop messages after shutdown
- Callers (NewAPIServer, NewSecureAPIServer) pass context.Background()
- New tests: TestWebSocketHubGracefulShutdown, TestWebSocketHubShutdownMethod

Closes #925

fix: replace X-Agent-Name header trust with JWT claims in 8 endpoints (#920)

All 8 vulnerable endpoints in api.go now require JWT authentication
instead of trusting the spoofable X-Agent-Name header:

- bookRoom: was falling back to "system" identity
- startConversation: was using header for rate limiting key
- createRule: was falling back to "admin" identity
- updateRule: was falling back to "admin" identity
- deleteRule: was falling back to "admin" identity
- setRuleEnabled: was falling back to "admin" identity
- resolveViolation: was falling back to "admin" identity
- sendWaterCoolerMessage: was using header for rate limiting

Each endpoint now extracts identity from GetAuthClaimsFromContext(),
matching the pattern already established in sendMessage (line 650).
Unauthenticated requests receive 401 Unauthorized.

Severity: Critical – prevents agent impersonation via header spoofing.

commit.message:
v0.1.9: fix root cause — add-on must call Supervisor discovery API

Every previous version listed 'discovery: [ha_basic_addon]' in config.json
but addon/main.py never called POST http://supervisor/discovery.

That declaration is an allowlist, not an auto-trigger. Without the actual
API call, Supervisor never notifies HA core, async_step_hassio is never
called, and the integration is never discovered — no matter how correct
the config_flow.py code is.

Fix: register_discovery() now called at startup before HTTP server starts.
  POST http://supervisor/discovery
  Authorization: Bearer {SUPERVISOR_TOKEN}
  Body: {service: ha_basic_addon, config: {host: 127.0.0.1, port: 8080}}

Supervisor assigns a UUID, notifies HA core, HA creates the config flow,
async_step_hassio fires, _set_confirm_only() surfaces the badge.

Also: remove ingress:true — ingress requires X-Ingress-Token validation
which was not implemented, causing silent HTTP response failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v0.1.7: add translations/en.json, fix entity unique_ids, two sensor entities

Critical fixes for discovery and entity registration:

1. Add translations/en.json (was the main discovery blocker)
   HA resolves config flow UI strings from translations/{lang}.json
   at runtime. strings.json is build-time only. Without this file
   the hassio_confirm step renders as raw keys and some HA versions
   silently refuse to show the flow at all.

2. Fix hacs.json iot_class: local_push -> local_polling
   Must match manifest.json. HACS uses this for categorisation.

3. Fix sensor unique_ids to use entry.entry_id
   Static domain string caused collision on reload/multi-instance.
   Now: {entry_id}_status and {entry_id}_uptime.

4. Fix AddEntitiesCallback import: helpers.entity -> entity_platform
5. Fix DeviceInfo import: helpers.entity -> device_registry

6. Expose two entities under one device card:
   - sensor.*_status (health text, extra attrs: greeting/path/ts)
   - sensor.*_uptime (float seconds, DURATION/TOTAL_INCREASING)
   Both use has_entity_name=True and share DeviceInfo(entry_id).

Fix dead pattern listeners by implementing proper event architecture

Fixes #872

**Problem:**
setupPatternListeners() in scene.js referenced window.officeBackend.onPatternStarted/onPatternEnded which existed in the preload API, but the backend's checkProximityInteractions() didn't emit pattern events. The scene was directly creating patterns and calling visual handlers, bypassing the event system entirely.

**Solution:**
1. Modified office-backend.js checkProximityInteractions() to:
   - Create proper pattern objects with id, type, location, duration
   - Store patterns in this.interactions map
   - Emit 'pattern-started' events that IPC forwards to renderer
   - Auto-end patterns after duration by calling endInteractionPattern()

2. Removed direct pattern creation in scene.js:
   - Deleted ~70 lines of manual PatternSelector calls
   - Removed duplicate awareness indicator suppression logic
   - Now relies on event listeners for pattern rendering

3. Enhanced setupPatternListeners() to:
   - Handle awareness indicator suppression when patterns start
   - Re-enable awareness indicators when patterns end
   - Added #872 reference to console logs

**Architecture:**
Backend emits events → IPC forwards → Preload exposes → Scene listens → Visuals render

This decouples the proximity check (backend concern) from visual rendering (frontend concern) and makes the event flow consistent with other interaction patterns.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

triggers, stale lock cleanup) are properly stopped when the app closes.

- Prevents orphaned timers on app shutdown
- Matches cleanup pattern used by activity feed and routines

commit.message:
🔒 Fix critical CORS security vulnerability (#803)

Replace wildcard CORS origin with configurable allowed origins to prevent
CSRF attacks on the office API server.

Changes:
- Replace AllowedOrigins: []string{"*"} with config-based origins
- Fix WebSocket CheckOrigin to validate against allowed origins list
- Add isAllowedOrigin() helper function for origin validation
- Update APIServer constructor to accept allowedOrigins parameter
- Add comprehensive origin validation test suite
- Update documentation to reflect secure implementation

Security Impact:
BEFORE: Any website could make authenticated requests and establish
WebSocket connections, enabling CSRF attacks and data exfiltration.

AFTER: Only explicitly configured origins (via ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var)
can make authenticated cross-origin requests.

Fixes: #803
Filed-by: carol (code review)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
